Bluetooth driver test functions for starting, stopping, and handling HCI passthrough and RF test modes.

# Purpose
This code is a C source file that provides a set of functions for testing Bluetooth driver functionalities. It includes functions to start and stop the Bluetooth driver test ([`bt_driver_test_start`](<#bt_driver_test_start>) and [`bt_driver_test_stop`](<#bt_driver_test_stop>)), enter HCI passthrough mode ([`bt_driver_test_enter_hci_passthrough`](<#bt_driver_test_enter_hci_passthrough>)), and handle HCI passthrough characters ([`bt_driver_test_handle_hci_passthrough_character`](<#bt_driver_test_handle_hci_passthrough_character>)). The file also contains functions to enter RF test mode ([`bt_driver_test_enter_rf_test_mode`](<#bt_driver_test_enter_rf_test_mode>)), set a spoof Bluetooth device address ([`bt_driver_test_set_spoof_address`](<#bt_driver_test_set_spoof_address>)), and perform self-tests on the Bluetooth driver and MFi chip ([`bt_driver_test_selftest`](<#bt_driver_test_selftest>) and [`bt_driver_test_mfi_chip_selftest`](<#bt_driver_test_mfi_chip_selftest>)). Additionally, it provides a function to perform a core dump of the Bluetooth driver ([`bt_driver_core_dump`](<#bt_driver_core_dump>)). The file is intended for use in testing and debugging Bluetooth driver implementations.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `bluetooth/bt_test.h`
